item.title: Canada Inflation Jumps to 2.4% as Iran War Drives Up Gas Prices
item.description: Canada’s inflation rate surged to 2.4% in March as the Iran war triggered a record increase in gasoline prices, though it came in slightly lower than economists expected.

item.title: Blackstone-Backed Jersey Mike’s Submits Confidential IPO Filing
item.description: Jersey Mike’s Subs, the sandwich chain with more than 3,000 locations, submitted a confidential filing with the US Securities and Exchange Commission for its initial public offering.

item.title: Brazil Economists Lift Selic 2026, 2027 Forecasts on Oil Spike
item.description: Brazil economists raised their forecasts for the benchmark interest rate for this year and next as an energy price spike from the war in Iran reverberates though Latin America’s biggest economy.

item.title: US Stock Futures Decline on Dimmed US-Iran Peace Talk Prospects
item.description: US stock-index futures fell before the bell on Monday as weekend developments in the war in the Middle East dimmed prospects on peace talks between the US and Iran.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for April 20, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures fall 0.5% as of 7:50 a.m. in New York after the US maintained a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and seized an Iranian ship.

item.title: Chaos in Hormuz Should Be a Reality Check for Oil Prices
item.description: Commodity flows from the Persian Gulf are back at a near-standstill, a reminder of the intractable nature of the war in Iran.

item.title: China Pushes Back Over Criticism of Its Giant Surpluses
item.description: At the IMF meetings, Beijing rejected criticism from others about its trade position.

item.title: Energy Services Set to Benefit Once Iran War Ends, Barclays Says
item.description: The European energy services sector faces a brighter future once the Middle East conflict de-escalates as a result of work that oil producers need to undertake to resolve disruption, according to Barclays Plc analysts.

item.title: Allbirds’ Move to AI Has Echoes of the Dot-Com Frenzy
item.description: The faltering shoe company says it will become an AI computing provider

item.title: Examining the Durability of the Deals Trump Negotiates
item.description: His strategy with Iran involves familiar moves aimed at maximizing leverage

item.title: UnitedHealth Isn’t Out of the Woods With $6 Billion Medicare Hit
item.description: UnitedHealth Group Inc.’s results Tuesday will reflect the full effect of Medicare payment changes set in motion three years ago that cost the company billions, cratering profits last year and blindsiding investors.

item.title: Fed Pick Warsh to Face Senate Grilling With Confirmation in Limbo
item.description: Kevin Warsh has for years accused the Federal Reserve of losing its way and said it’s in desperate need of “regime change.”

item.title: USA Rare Earth to Buy Brazil’s Serra Verde in $2.8 Billion Deal
item.description: USA Rare Earth Inc. agreed to acquire Brazil’s Serra Verde Group in a cash-and-stock transaction, adding to a string of recent deals in the industry.

item.title: German Banks Aren’t Panicking Over Mythos AI Threat, Sewing Says
item.description: Germany’s banks are well-prepared for heightened cyber risks as Anthropic’s new artificial intelligence model sparks global fears of a new era for computer hacks, said Deutsche Bank AG Chief Executive Officer Christian Sewing.

item.title: India’s RBI Eases Some Curbs on Banks’ Forex Market Trades
item.description: India’s central bank eased some restrictions on banks’ offshore currency market transactions that it had imposed earlier this month to shore up the rupee.

item.title: AST Drops as Blue Origin Rocket Places Satellite in Wrong Orbit
item.description: AST SpaceMobile Inc. shares plunged in early trading on Monday after Blue Origin’s flagship New Glenn rocket failed to correctly place a payload it was carrying for the Texas-based satellite networking company into its intended orbit.

item.title: Finnish Defense Technology Duo Are Said to Weigh Helsinki IPOs
item.description: Two Finnish defense technology providers are putting together plans to go public, looking to tap equity markets as Europe ramps up military spending.

item.title: Honeywell Said Close to Selling Unit to Brady for $1.4 Billion
item.description: Honeywell International Inc. is nearing a deal to divest its productivity solutions and services business to industrial manufacturer Brady Corp., according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Google Eyes New Chips to Speed Up AI Results, Challenging Nvidia
item.description: The company aims to build on its momentum after inking deals with Meta and Anthropic.

item.title: JPMorgan and Citi Square Off on the Next Payments Frontier
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. and Citigroup Inc. have long competed for dominance in global payments, processing trillions of dollars worth of flows from corporates moving money across borders. That rivalry is now extending to new payment rails.

item.title: Drop in UK Bond Sales to Limit Political Risk Selloff, Citi Says
item.description: A drop in the UK’s planned bond sales may give the debt some support to counter a selloff driven by political risks, according to Citigroup Inc.

item.title: Pernod Ricard Is Said to Begin IPO Process for India Unit
item.description: Pernod Ricard SA, the maker of Absolut vodka and Chivas Regal whisky, has begun work on a potential initial public offering of its Indian business, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: China’s Tencent Invests in Kazakhstan Fintech Company Kaspi.kz
item.description: Chinese technology giant Tencent Holdings Ltd. made its debut investment into Kazakhstan’s fintech sector with the acquisition of a stake in Kaspi.kz JSC.

item.title: Huawei Unfurls Foldable Phone While Apple Stages China Comeback
item.description: Huawei Technologies Co. unveiled its latest foldable phone on Monday, betting on the novel format to flesh out its lineup and fend off resurgent competition in China from Apple Inc.

item.title: Bond Bulls Look to Warsh for Next Leg of Rally
item.description: Dovish comments could fuel another leg down in Treasury yields.

item.title: Philippines Outlook Cut to Negative by Fitch on Growth Risks
item.description: Fitch Ratings revised its outlook on the Philippines’ credit rating to negative from stable, saying the decline in public investment has created risks to economic growth.

item.title: Kenya Trims Tax Revenue Goal, Ramps Up Borrowing
item.description: Kenya cut its tax revenue target for the fiscal year through June and increased its borrowing plans, according to Treasury Secretary John Mbadi.

item.title: Singapore Firms Feel Energy Cost Squeeze, Most Hold Off Job Cuts
item.description: Singapore companies are exploring adjustments as rising energy prices begin to bite and concerns over labor costs mount.

item.title: Treasuries Slip as Renewed Ceasefire Tensions Lift Oil Prices
item.description: Treasuries retreated slightly on Monday as the US seizure of an Iranian ship dampened hopes of de-escalation in the Middle East conflict and sent oil prices higher.

item.title: China’s SF Holding Seeks Up to $1 Billion From Convertible Bonds
item.description: SF Holding Co., China’s biggest express-delivery firm, is considering raising as much as about $1 billion via a convertible bond offering in Hong Kong that may come alongside a follow-on share placement, according to people familiar with the matter.
